data.title: Fortuna Mining Lifts Reserves 15% as Séguéla Sunbird Deposit Grows
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Fortuna Mining Corp ( (TSE:FVI) ) has shared an update.

On April 23, 2026, Fortuna Mining Corp. reported a 15% year-over-year increase in consolidated Proven and Probable mineral reserves to about 3.0 million gold-equivalent ounces, after production depletion, underpinned mainly by upgrades at the Séguéla mine. The company also posted a 56% rise in Measured and Indicated resources to 2.1 million gold-equivalent ounces and a 4% increase in Inferred resources to 2.2 million gold-equivalent ounces, reflecting strong drilling results across its portfolio.

At Séguéla’s Sunbird underground deposit, reserves rose 34% to 539,000 ounces of gold and Inferred resources climbed 55% to 417,000 ounces, driven by infill and exploration drilling completed in the second half of 2025. Fortuna said the Diamba Sud project feasibility study remains on track to support an initial reserve declaration and a construction decision by the end of the current quarter, signaling potential future production growth that could further strengthen the company’s regional footprint and long-term mine life.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:FVI) stock is a Buy with a C$14.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Fortuna Mining Corp stock, see the TSE:FVI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FVI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FVI is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by improved financial performance (material margin and cash flow strength with conservative leverage) and a constructive, growth-focused earnings call backed by strong liquidity. Valuation is supportive with a low P/E, while technical signals are only moderately positive and remain largely neutral on momentum.

To see Spark’s full report on FVI stock, click here.

More about Fortuna Mining Corp

Fortuna Mining Corp. is a precious metals producer with operating gold and silver mines and development projects in West Africa and Latin America. Its portfolio includes the Séguéla mine in Côte d’Ivoire, the Lindero gold mine and Arizaro project in Argentina, the Caylloma silver mine in Peru, and the Diamba Sud gold project in Senegal, focused on growing gold-equivalent reserves and resources.

Average Trading Volume: 1,210,962

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: C$4.2B

For a thorough assessment of FVI stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Expedia ( (EXPE) ) just unveiled an update.

Expedia Group announced on April 23, 2026, that Derek Andersen will become chief financial officer effective May 11, 2026, succeeding Scott Schenkel, who is stepping down after a 16‑month tenure and will depart the company on May 16, 2026. The company said Schenkel’s exit is not related to any disagreement over operations or accounting, and highlighted his role in strengthening the balance sheet and expanding margins.

Andersen, the former CFO of Snap Inc. with prior senior finance roles at Amazon and Fox Interactive Media, will lead Expedia Group’s global finance organization from Seattle and report to CEO Ariane Gorin. His pay package includes a $1 million base salary, up to $2.5 million in signing bonuses, significant relocation support, severance protections, and a $17 million initial equity grant, underscoring Expedia’s bid to secure seasoned tech‑sector financial leadership as it pursues growth and profitability in the competitive online travel market.

The most recent analyst rating on (EXPE) stock is a Hold with a $255.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Expedia stock, see the EXPE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on EXPE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, EXPE is a Neutral.

EXPE scores well on profitability/cash generation and upbeat guidance for 2026, but the overall score is held back by elevated leverage and a weakened technical setup (trading below key moving averages). Valuation provides limited support with a higher P/E and modest dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on EXPE stock, click here.

More about Expedia

Expedia Group, Inc. is a global online travel marketplace that connects travelers, partners, and advertisers through brands including Expedia, Hotels.com, and Vrbo. Leveraging proprietary technology and first‑party data, the company offers consumer travel services and operates one of the largest B2B travel businesses and an advertising network in more than 70 countries.

data.title: Clean Energy Fuels Appoints Clay Corbus as New CEO
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Clean Energy Fuels ( (CLNE) ) has shared an update.

On April 23, 2026, Clean Energy Fuels announced that longtime executive Clay (Barclay) Corbus was appointed President and Chief Executive Officer, effective April 22, 2026, and joined the board, succeeding co-founder Andrew Littlefair after his three-decade tenure as CEO. The leadership change formalizes Corbus’s rise from senior roles in strategy and renewable fuels and is structured with a new employment agreement that includes a multi-year term, performance-based bonus opportunity, equity incentives, and enhanced severance protections, signaling the board’s commitment to stability and growth during a critical phase in the alternative fuels market.

Littlefair, who helped build Clean Energy into a major RNG provider, resigned from his executive role on the April 22 transition date but will remain on the board and serve as a paid government-relations consultant under a three-year agreement. His consulting package, including a substantial annual retainer, potential 2026 bonus, benefits stipend, life insurance support, and a multi-year restricted stock grant, is designed to keep his industry relationships and policy expertise aligned with Clean Energy as it navigates regulatory dynamics and seeks to expand its renewable natural gas platform.

The most recent analyst rating on (CLNE) stock is a Buy with a $4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Clean Energy Fuels stock, see the CLNE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CLNE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CLNE is a Neutral.

CLNE scores mid-range primarily because stronger cash flow and a significantly improved balance sheet are offset by sharply weaker profitability and ongoing losses. Earnings call guidance and execution milestones (higher adjusted EBITDA outlook, cost reductions, volume growth targets, and no new borrowing) modestly support the score, while technicals are largely neutral and valuation remains constrained by negative earnings and no indicated dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on CLNE stock, click here.

More about Clean Energy Fuels

Clean Energy Fuels Corp., listed on Nasdaq as CLNE, is the largest U.S. provider of renewable natural gas for the transportation market, operating a wide network of fueling stations across the U.S. and Canada. The company focuses on decarbonizing transportation by producing and distributing RNG from methane captured at sources such as dairy farms, serving fleets ranging from airport shuttles to city buses and heavy-duty trucks.

Its business model centers on supplying low-carbon fuel and related infrastructure to commercial and municipal vehicle fleets seeking to cut greenhouse gas emissions. Through its RNG production facilities and distribution platform, Clean Energy aims to benefit from the ongoing transition of the heavy-duty transportation sector toward alternative, cleaner fuels.

Average Trading Volume: 1,376,779

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $524.4M

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Lsi Industries ( (LYTS) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 23, 2026, LSI Industries reported fiscal third-quarter 2026 results for the period ended March 31, 2026, highlighting a 14% year-over-year rise in net sales to $150.5 million, including six days of contribution from its newly acquired Royston Group, with net sales excluding Royston up 9%. Net income was $2.1 million versus adjusted net income of $9.6 million, driven by acquisition-related non-recurring items, while adjusted EBITDA grew 34% to $15 million and free cash flow, excluding acquisition-related items, reached $11.8 million, supporting a quarterly dividend of $0.05 per share payable in May 2026.

The quarter underscored LSI’s strategy of building a one-stop, branded solutions platform through acquisitions, including the March 24, 2026 completion of Royston and an underwritten equity offering that increased diluted shares outstanding, leaving net debt at 2.7 times trailing pro forma adjusted EBITDA and about $100 million in liquidity. Management reported particularly strong performance in the Display Solutions segment, with 23% sales growth and margin expansion led by the grocery and refueling/convenience store verticals, rising order intake and backlog, and a new program award exceeding $5 million from the largest c-store chain in North America, reinforcing LSI’s positioning as a partner of choice for major retail brands.

Ardagh Metal Packaging ( (AMBP) ) has provided an update.

Ardagh Metal Packaging S.A. has filed its Form 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for April 2026, furnishing unaudited consolidated interim financial statements for the quarter ended March 31, 2026. The filing, dated April 23, 2026, includes full income, cash flow and balance sheet data and incorporates the report by reference into an existing shelf registration.

For the first quarter of 2026, Ardagh Metal Packaging reported revenue of $1.504 billion, up from $1.268 billion a year earlier, while narrowing its basic and diluted loss per share to $0.01 from $0.02. Despite higher operating profit, the group remained loss-making due to net finance costs, and the detailed quarterly disclosure will help investors reassess its leverage profile, earnings trajectory and positioning in the competitive beverage can market.

The score is held back primarily by balance-sheet risk (high debt and negative equity) and thin bottom-line profitability, despite solid recent cash generation. Management detailed the company’s mission-driven focus on client service, operational efficiency and associate engagement, underscored by high Net Promoter Scores, improving employee survey results, significant charitable giving and recognition in rankings for top regional banks, community banks, workplaces and corporate philanthropy.

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from ConnectM Technology Solutions ( (CNTM) ).

On April 23, 2026, ConnectM Technology Solutions, Inc. announced it is exploring two potential capital raises ahead of a planned uplisting to a national securities exchange. The company reported that in 2025 it achieved record revenue of $35.8 million and 93% gross profit growth, and it is aiming in 2026 to reach $75 million in revenue and generate positive EBITDA while scaling its AI‑powered platform of more than 130,000 connected assets.

ConnectM has engaged Moody Capital Solutions, Inc. to run a coordinated testing‑the‑waters process for a dual‑track structure consisting of a Rule 506(c) private placement for accredited investors and a Regulation A rights offering for existing shareholders. Management views this mix of common stock, dividend‑yielding preferred stock, warrants, and convertible preferred as a capital‑efficient, shareholder‑aligned alternative to traditional equity financing, designed to bridge a perceived valuation gap and support the company’s targeted NYSE American uplisting.

Spark’s Take on CNTM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CNTM is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance—large losses, severe cash burn, and high balance-sheet risk—despite strong revenue growth. Technical indicators are modestly supportive in the near term, while valuation is constrained by negative earnings and no stated dividend. Corporate events add some upside from strategic expansion but are tempered by dilution/leverage risk from convertible financing.

To see Spark’s full report on CNTM stock, click here.

More about ConnectM Technology Solutions

ConnectM Technology Solutions, Inc. is a constellation of technology-driven businesses focused on powering the modern energy economy. Through its Owned Service Network, Managed Solutions, Logistics, and Transportation segments, the company delivers AI-powered electrification, distributed energy, last‑mile delivery, and industrial IoT solutions globally, supported by its Keen Labs subsidiary’s energy storage, smart heat pump, and connected vehicle platforms.

Keen Labs develops the AI, control, and energy intelligence platforms underpinning ConnectM’s offerings, including industrial IoT hardware and the Hi‑C and Hi‑E energy storage lines. These technologies are integrated via software to optimize performance across fleets, facilities, and distributed energy assets, positioning ConnectM as a vertically integrated player in AI-driven clean energy infrastructure.

data.title: Honeywell Realigns Segments, Updates Automation Reporting Structure
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Honeywell International ( (HON) ).

Honeywell has recast its historical segment information for the year ended December 31, 2025 to reflect a 2026 realignment that created a new Process Automation and Technology segment and reshaped its Industrial Automation segment. The move combines core Honeywell Process Solutions offerings and UOP into the new unit, eliminates separate reporting of Energy and Sustainability Solutions following the October 30, 2025 spin-off of Advanced Materials, and leaves Aerospace Technologies, Building Automation, Process Automation and Technology, and Industrial Automation as the company’s reportable segments.

The company is updating prior disclosures, including business descriptions, risk factors, management discussion and segment financial data, solely to align with the new segment structure and a new revenue disaggregation by business model in automation-related segments. Honeywell emphasized that the realignment and recast do not alter previously reported consolidated financial statements or cash flows, signaling a reporting change aimed at better reflecting its evolving portfolio and segment leadership rather than a revision of underlying financial performance.

Ardagh Metal Packaging ( (AMBP) ) has shared an announcement.

Ardagh Metal Packaging reported first-quarter 2026 results on April 23, showing revenue up 19% year on year to $1.5 billion and Adjusted EBITDA up 15% to $179 million, despite a 1% decline in global beverage can shipments. The group maintained a small net loss of $5 million, but improved loss per share to $0.01 and kept its quarterly dividend at $0.10 per ordinary share.

Regional performance was mixed, with Americas Adjusted EBITDA slipping 2% to $104 million amid aluminum supply chain disruptions and higher costs, while Europe’s Adjusted EBITDA surged 53% to $75 million on stronger input cost recovery, FX tailwinds and favorable volume mix. The company highlighted liquidity of $488 million, refinanced and upsized its asset-based lending facility to $450 million maturing in 2031, upheld its full-year 2026 Adjusted EBITDA guidance of $750–775 million, and noted a U.S. court jury verdict on April 6 awarding it about $175 million in damages in a contract dispute with Boston Beer, underscoring both operational resilience and legal upside for stakeholders.

data.title: TruBridge to Be Acquired by IKS in All-Cash Deal
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from TruBridge ( (TBRG) ).

On April 23, 2026, TruBridge agreed to be acquired by Inventurus Knowledge Solutions’ U.S. subsidiary, IKS, in an all‑cash deal valuing TruBridge at $26.25 per share, with TruBridge to become a wholly owned subsidiary upon completion. The transaction, approved by both companies’ boards and backed by voting agreements covering about 27% of TruBridge’s stock, is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026 subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals, including Hart‑Scott‑Rodino clearance.

The merger pairs TruBridge’s revenue cycle and EHR systems for rural and community hospitals with IKS’s AI‑enabled care enablement platform, creating a scaled health‑tech provider serving more than 2,000 organizations and 150,000 clinicians. The combined company is positioned to deepen its presence in rural healthcare by offering more advanced, data‑driven workflows and financial support, potentially strengthening local hospital sustainability while IKS expands its U.S. footprint, funded primarily through new term‑loan debt.

More about TruBridge

TruBridge, Inc., listed on Nasdaq, provides healthcare technology, services and strategic expertise tailored to rural and community hospitals in the United States. Its portfolio centers on revenue cycle management, electronic health records and analytics, supporting more than 1,500 clients and helping local hospitals remain financially resilient and independent.

Inventurus Knowledge Solutions’ U.S. arm, IKS, is a care enablement specialist that combines agentic AI with human expertise to streamline clinical, operational and administrative workflows. By integrating TruBridge’s systems of record with its AI-driven “system of action,” IKS aims to offer a more intelligent, connected platform across ambulatory and acute care settings.

The latest update is out from Pool ( (POOL) ).

On April 23, 2026, Pool Corporation reported first-quarter 2026 net sales of $1.14 billion, up 6% year-on-year, driven by resilient maintenance demand, strong equipment sales and a gradual recovery in discretionary categories such as building materials. Gross profit rose while gross margin slipped 20 basis points to 29.0% due to a higher mix of equipment and early buy orders, with operating income up 7% to $82.6 million and diluted EPS increasing 2% to $1.45, or 8% on an adjusted basis excluding lower tax benefits.

The company increased inventory 14% to $1.7 billion ahead of the pool season and lifted total debt to $1.2 billion, largely to fund $349 million of share repurchases over the past 12 months, while operating cash flow was slightly lower than a year earlier. Management confirmed its full-year 2026 earnings guidance, citing greenfield investments, operating expense leverage and its broad sales center network and technology platform as supports for profitable growth and competitive positioning in the outdoor living market.

data.title: Alpha Tau Treats First European Pancreatic Cancer Patient With Alpha DaRT in ACAPELLA Trial
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Alpha Tau Medical Ltd ( (DRTS) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 23, 2026, Alpha Tau Medical announced that it has successfully treated the first European pancreatic cancer patient with its Alpha DaRT therapy in the ACAPELLA multicenter trial in France. The study targets inoperable locally advanced pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma patients who have completed first-line mFOLFIRINOX chemotherapy, addressing a large European population with no established standard consolidation therapy.

Conducted at CHU Grenoble Alpes, the procedure marked the first use of Alpha DaRT for pancreatic cancer in Europe and is part of a broader strategy to build global clinical evidence, alongside an ongoing U.S. multicenter trial in the same indication. Management and investigators characterized the milestone as a key inflection point for expanding Alpha DaRT in pancreatic cancer, with ACAPELLA designed primarily to assess safety and secondarily to track tumor response, survival, resectability, and quality-of-life outcomes over a year of follow-up.

The most recent analyst rating on (DRTS) stock is a Buy with a $9.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Alpha Tau Medical Ltd stock, see the DRTS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on DRTS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DRTS is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held down by very weak financial performance (no revenue, widening losses, and significant ongoing cash burn), partially offset by a comparatively improved balance sheet and moderate leverage. Technicals are neutral-to-slightly constructive on longer-term moving averages, while valuation remains challenged due to negative earnings and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on DRTS stock, click here.

More about Alpha Tau Medical Ltd

Alpha Tau Medical Ltd. is an Israeli oncology therapeutics company focused on developing and potentially commercializing Alpha DaRT, an intratumoral alpha-radiation therapy for solid tumors. The proprietary Diffusing Alpha-emitters Radiation Therapy platform aims to deliver highly potent, localized alpha irradiation that destroys tumors while largely sparing surrounding healthy tissue, positioning the firm in the advanced radiotherapy segment of cancer care.
